transcript: Yeah, this is David Donnaruma. I left a message for you—lesterday [sic], I think it was—uh, for— You told me to call back 'cause you know I… I’m not in California, I’m in Idaho. I asked you to send me the—
You shoulda gave me one when I was there. Woulda been easier. I didn’t need the new one. I needed— This is the la—last year’s issue. I didn’t care about the new one. [unclear]

I wouldn’t— Coulda got— I wouldn’t have to keep calling and begging for it. 'Cause you were 'posed to call me back. If you call me ba— Or you got the information, you are sendin' it. I need to know if you’re sending it or not.
’Cause when I come in June I’m gonna... um, ask for it then. You’d have to give it to me then. I’m gonna do my errands again and I’m gonna swing by there again. And—

I’m still waiting so call me back. And if I don’t pick up my cellphone, leave a message. My cellphone’s not gonna work right now because I ha— I’m havin’ some money problems. Uh, I’m waiting for some money to come in. I don’t know when at all my phone—
I live in a motel. I have to live here ’til I find an apartment. Little money problem. But if you call room number two-oh-seven—that’s my room number.
Thank you for calling the Budget Inn in downtown Boise. If you know the guest’s room number you are trying to reach, you may enter it now.
Uh, I was transferred— I was transferred to you, uh… to pay for an order. Are you expecting this?
A "order"? What are you talkin’ about?—
Yes, one of your friends, uh… It’s just a pizza place. Someone called, made an order, and said they would connect me to the person that was paying.
I don’t—! I don’t—! That’s— You got the wrong person. I never ordered a pizza.—
Alright, that’s no problem, man. That’s no problem. Have a good night.—
’Kay. Don’t call here again. 'Cause I’m gonna call the police.
Um… I’m told that you’re gonna pay for an… a dinner? Or an...
Um, this is Oriental Express. And, uh…—
What are you talkin’ about? I didn’t order no pizza!
This is Chao with Oriental Express—downtown Boise.
No, this is a res—[to "Woman(2)"] Wha—what is this? Did he call?
No, his, like, friend called. This person called saying that David—whoever’s talking…
What’s the—? What’s the name of this place?
This is Oriental Express—downtown Boise and Lee is calling to order? And… [speaks quietly to "Woman(2)"]
And you gonna pick it up. Is that true?—
Pay, pay, not pick it up.—
Oh, you not the person. Okay! Thank you!—
Uh, my— It— Yeah. Stop calling here! 'Cause I’m gonna call the police.
Hi! Uh, this is Amy with Romeo’s Pizza and Pasta.
I never ordered anything. Stop calling! You know my last name at all? 'Cause I—I didn’t— This isn’t—
A gentleman named Lee just placed an order with me and he was transferring me over to your room. He said you gonna be the one purchasing it.
No, you’ve got the wrong David! Did he say whose last name it is?
I don’t know anybody in this place.
Okay! I’ll d— I’ll call and I’ll [unclear].—

I don’t hang around with low-class people. Uh… I don’t ha— I don’t— This person’s, uh… is not all there. He must be on drugs. Uh… because [laughs] I don’t hang around with these type of people. I don’t— I don’t’ even mingle in this motel. Only person that knows me here is next door to me. Only person I talk to. The rest I don’t talk to. Because they’re—

they’re not my type of people. They’re wh*te trash. Uh… Uh… So… 'cause I— The people I know are in show business. That’s the only people I know. Big actors and directors—that’s who I hang around with. So I don’t hang around with low class people. So don’t call here anymore 'cause I—I— The police is gonna come down next. 'Cause they keep doin' this and [unclear] the other day, uh… three to four times. Callin' my room. Um, it’s the wrong person. There’s nobody— There’s nobody— [laughs] I mean, I’m David but it’s not— I don’t pay for people’s food. Okay? I haven’t— I’m— I have my own money. I don’t pay for people. Anyways. Only person I pay for is in L.A., okay? My friends. And they pay for me. So… tell that guy to… get the right person or get off the drugs or call the police on 'im. Okay? Bye.
